questions.question: Differentiate between Scope 1 and Scope 2 carbon emissions. Provide one example for each.
expectedPoints: Scope 1 emissions are direct emissions from sources owned or controlled by the entity (e.g., fuel combustion in company-owned vehicles).

Scope 2 emissions are indirect emissions from the generation of purchased electricity, steam, heating, or cooling consumed by the entity (e.g., emissions from the power plant supplying electricity to an office building).

questions.question: Discuss the major strategies that a national government can adopt to mitigate the carbon footprint of its economy. (10 marks)
expectedPoints: Implementation of Carbon Pricing: Explain carbon tax and cap-and-trade systems as tools to incentivize emission reductions by putting a financial cost on pollution.
Regulatory Standards: Enforcing stringent emission norms for industries and fuel efficiency standards for vehicles.
Investment in Green Infrastructure: Funding public transportation networks, renewable energy projects (solar, wind farms), and smart grids.
Research & Development Support: Providing grants and subsidies for innovation in green technologies like carbon capture and storage (CCS) and energy storage.
International Policy Alignment: Participating in and adhering to global agreements like the Paris Agreement to ensure coordinated action.
